meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop searching for project ideas. Learn how to validate SaaS pricing, build lean MVPs, and ship revenue-generating products using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop chasing 'perfect' ideas. Instead, build a micro-SaaS around a high-intent pricing model. Identify a manual workflow, automate it with an AI wrapper, and charge a monthly subscription. By starting with the pricing structure first, you force yourself to build features that provide actual, quantifiable business value.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You are stuck in 'idea paralysis,' waiting for a lightning bolt of inspiration that never strikes. But the most successful founders don't wait for ideas; they hunt for inefficiencies and attach a price tag to the solution.

The secret isn't a better idea; it's a better framework. By shifting your focus from 'what should I build' to 'what is someone willing to pay for,' you transform your development process from a hobbyist's daydream into a professional revenue engine. Let's build your first profitable asset.

steps.title: Identify High-Value Inefficiencies
steps.description: Find a niche audience spending hours on manual data processing. Your goal is to find a task where the 'Time Saved' multiplied by the 'Hourly Rate' exceeds $50/month.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze common SaaS pain points for B2B logistics coordinators and generate 5 micro-SaaS ideas that save at least 2 hours of manual data entry daily.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Research current market gaps in B2B logistics automation and output a list of 5 validated SaaS niches.'

steps.title: Define the Pricing Tiers
steps.description: Don't guess; structure. Create a Freemium tier for lead generation and a Pro tier that solves the core pain point. Use value-based pricing, not cost-plus.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a pricing strategy table for a B2B SaaS tool. Include a 'Starter' tier for lead capture and a 'Pro' tier focused on API-driven automation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Write a pricing strategy document for a SaaS tool targeting logistics, including tiers, feature gating, and value-based rationale.'

steps.title: Scaffold the MVP Architecture
steps.description: Use modern stacks to ensure speed-to-market. We focus on a Next.js frontend and a serverless backend to keep infrastructure costs near zero.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a project scaffold for a Next.js application with Stripe integration and Supabase authentication for a SaaS MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Scaffold a new Next.js project with Supabase and Stripe Checkout integration.'

steps.title: Implement Stripe Payment Hooks
steps.description: The most critical step: if you can't collect money, you don't have a business. Automate the subscription lifecycle immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the Stripe webhook handler logic for a Next.js API route that handles subscription creation and user access provisioning.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a secure Stripe webhook handler for subscription events in a Next.js backend.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Ship to Vercel and get your first user. The goal is not perfection; it is the first dollar in your bank account.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment checklist for a SaaS MVP, covering domain DNS, environment variables, and production-ready Stripe webhooks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a production deployment checklist for a Vercel-hosted SaaS application.'

pro_tips: Never build a 'general' tool. The riches are in the specifics—target a sub-niche so small your marketing costs drop to zero.
Charge early. If users won't pay for the beta, they won't pay for the final product. Pivot before you code.
Use 'Feature Gating' as your primary marketing tool. Give away the utility, charge for the volume or the speed.
The first version should be an 'AI Wrapper' that solves one problem perfectly. Complexity is the enemy of early revenue.
Always include a 'Contact Sales' button for the highest tier, even if it just emails you. It makes your brand look enterprise-ready immediately.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, reach out to 50 potential users on LinkedIn or Twitter. Do not ask for feedback; ask for a pilot subscription. Your goal is to reach $100 MRR within 30 days.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the architectural blueprint to stop dreaming and start shipping. Every day you wait is a day of lost revenue. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
